steps.title: Identify what constitutes evidence
steps.content: The notes define a specific requirement for a true circumnavigation: it must begin and end in the same port. To provide evidence that Randmaa's journey was a true circumnavigation, the student must demonstrate that he met this requirement.

steps.title: Match the requirement to the choices
steps.content:
Choice B shows Randmaa began and ended in Les Sables d'Olonne and explicitly tags this as “one of the requirements for a true circumnavigation”—completing the logical proof.

The others either cite the requirement then mention unrelated facts (A), or claim it was a true circumnavigation without showing the requirement was satisfied (C, D).

That matches choice B — B is correct.
